case_name: Gary Horndeski M.D. v. Cheryl Price

summary.brief: Gary Horndeski M.D. v. Cheryl Price. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: Appellee Cheryl Price sued Appellant Gary Horndeski M.D. for negligence arising from the breast reconstruction surgery he performed on her.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On January 4, 2021, Price filed a health care liability suit against Dr. Horndeski alleging he 'was negligent in providing appropriate and timely medical care and treatment to [her] while she was his patient and breached his duty to provide the standard of care in restoring and treating [her].'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In my opinion, the procedure recommended and carried out by Dr. Horndeski on March 5, 2019 departed from and was below the accepted standard of care in the following respects.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: Based upon my experience, training, practice and review of literature, the accepted standard of care for a plastic surgeon presented with the history and examination of Cheryl Price as set forth within Dr. Horndeski’s records is to perform no reconstructive procedure whatsoever.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: We affirm the trial court’s order denying Dr. Horndeski’s motion to dismiss with prejudice Price’s negligence claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Although the Amended Report could have provided a more robust discussion of Dr. Lapuerta’s opinion on the causal relationship between Dr. Horndeski’s breach and Price’s harm, we nevertheless consider it sufficient.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Patient presented for care and treatment to remove excess skin under each arm after breast cancer surgery
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented for care and treatment to remove excess skin under each arm after breast cancer surgery. (E002, E003)
past_medical_history: Bilateral mastectomies followed by delayed breast cancer reconstruction with implants in 1999
Developed exposure and had bilateral implants removed

requestables.name: Surgical Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation regarding the suitability of the Bellasoma procedure for breast reconstruction.

reveal.clinical_significance: Procedure was contraindicated due to patient's medical history
reveal.decision_impact: Should have prompted reconsideration of surgical approach

timeline_phases.name: Pre-Surgical Evaluation
timeline_phases.description: Evaluation and decision-making regarding breast reconstruction surgery

timeline_phases.name: Bellasoma Procedure
timeline_phases.description: Execution of the Bellasoma breast reconstruction surgery

timeline_phases.name: Post-Operative Complications
timeline_phases.description: Management of complications following the Bellasoma procedure

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with a history of severe post-surgical complications seeking breast reconstruction
decision_points.prompt: Should the Bellasoma procedure be performed?

options.clinical_reasoning: Procedure was contraindicated due to patient's history

options.description: Refrain from performing any breast reconstruction surgery
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient's history of complications contraindicates further surgery

expected_action_court.description: Surgery should not have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established surgery was contraindicated

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on evidence E003-E004, the procedure was contraindicated
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Surgery should have been avoided due to patient's history

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed severe complications including abscess formation and bilateral open breast wounds

key_findings: Breach of duty in performing contraindicated surgery
Causation established - surgery led to severe complications

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Avoiding surgery would have prevented complications

complications.complication: Abscess formation, bilateral open breast wounds
